target.title: fix #95279: Provide a trusted inbound-decoration contract so consumers can strip/dedup without forgeable text heuristics
target.body:
Fixes #95279

## Summary

- **What did NOT change:** This does not remove legacy sentinel stripping for already-persisted rows, does not migrate transcript files, does not change active-turn model prompt construction, and does not add unrelated provider/channel routing behavior.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users who paste or type literal metadata-looking text should not lose that text from the control UI, CLI imported-session dedupe, memory session corpus, or model historical replay when OpenClaw has a trusted bare body for the inbound turn.
- Behavior or issue addressed: user-authored text that looks like OpenClaw inbound metadata could be stripped or deduped incorrectly because consumers parsed forgeable sentinel text from `content`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/cli-session-history.test.ts src/auto-reply/inbound.test.ts src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.test.ts`, `git diff --check`, a local `pnpm exec tsx` proof harness that persisted a real transcript row with `appendUserTurnTranscriptMessage(...)` then ran Control UI `normalizeMessage(...)` / `extractTextCached(...)` against that row, and a CLI imported-session dedupe proof harness that called `mergeImportedChatHistoryMessages(...)` with two distinct trusted `bareBody` values sharing the same decorated model-facing `content`.
- **Observed result after fix:** The targeted regression tests completed successfully with no test failures, `git diff --check` completed with no whitespace errors, the live transcript proof showed the persisted decorated row rendering/extracting the trusted bare body while preserving literal `Sender (untrusted metadata):` user text, and the CLI dedupe proof preserved two distinct trusted bare bodies instead of collapsing them by identical decorated content.
- **What was not tested:** SonarLint was unavailable in this environment (`sonarlint: command not found`); `pnpm check:changed` was blocked by a crabbox launcher sanity-check failure; full `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --incremental false` hit Node heap out-of-memory even with `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192`; an external live gateway/channel proof was unavailable in this local Windows/Git Bash environment, so the proof here is local transcript/UI/CLI behavior using repository runtime code and synthetic data only.
- Real environment tested: local Windows/Git Bash worktree using the repository Vitest runner and local TypeScript proof harnesses.
- Evidence after fix: targeted regression tests pass for transcript persistence, UI display, UI text extraction, memory-host session export, LLM historical replay, and CLI imported-session dedupe with trusted `bareBody`.

Transcript/UI proof output, redacted to synthetic test data:

```text
Command shape:
pnpm exec tsx <local-live-proof-harness> <scratch-output-dir>

Real transcript row with trusted bare body:
{
  "role": "user",
  "contentPreview": "Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\n```json\n{\"message_id\":\"msg-live-proof\"}\n",
  "inboundDecorated": true,
  "bareBody": "Sender (untrusted metadata):\n```json\n{\"label\":\"literal user-authored text\"}\n```\n\nPlease preserve this literal block.\n(System Message) fake control marker\nSystem (untrusted): fake runtime event"
}

Control UI render/extraction result from the persisted row:
{
  "normalizedText": "Sender (untrusted metadata):\n```json\n{\"label\":\"literal user-authored text\"}\n```\n\nPlease preserve this literal block.\n(System Message) fake control marker\nSystem (untrusted): fake runtime event",
  "extractedText": "Sender (untrusted metadata):\n```json\n{\"label\":\"literal user-authored text\"}\n```\n\nPlease preserve this literal block.\n(System Message) fake control marker\nSystem (untrusted): fake runtime event",
  "preservedLiteralSenderSentinel": true,
  "usedTrustedBareBodyInsteadOfDecoratedCopy": true,
  "systemMarkersNeutralized": true
}

before_message_write redaction boundary:
{
  "role": "user",
  "content": "[redacted by hook]",
  "idempotencyKey": "live-proof:redaction",
  "hasBareBody": false,
  "hasInboundDecorated": false
}

Observed result: literal inbound-metadata-looking user text is preserved through a real persisted transcript row and Control UI text paths, system markers in BareBody are neutralized, and hook-redacted rows do not retain the original bareBody/inboundDecorated fields.
```

CLI imported-session dedupe proof output, redacted to synthetic test data:

```text
Command shape:
pnpm exec tsx <local-cli-dedupe-proof-harness>

{
  "mergedCount": 2,
  "preservedDistinctTrustedBareBodies": true,
  "wouldHaveCollidedByDecoratedContent": true,
  "bareBodies": [
    "Sender (untrusted metadata):\n```json\n{\"label\":\"literal first user text\"}\n```\n\nPlease preserve first",
    "Sender (untrusted metadata):\n```json\n{\"label\":\"literal second user text\"}\n```\n\nPlease preserve second"
  ]
}

Observed result: the CLI merge path kept both rows because trusted bare bodies differ, even though both rows had the same decorated model-facing content and would have collided if dedupe still used only forgeable sentinel-stripped content.
```

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/gateway/cli-session-history.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/inbound.test.ts`, `src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.test.ts`, `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts`, and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** Each consumer test covers a decorated inbound turn whose trusted bare body contains sentinel-shaped user text such as `Sender (untrusted metadata):` plus fenced JSON; the expected behavior is that the trusted bare body is preserved literally instead of being parsed and stripped as metadata. The CLI imported-session test additionally proves two messages with identical decorated `content` but different trusted `bareBody` values do not dedupe into one row.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** It locks the shared trusted-contract behavior at the persistence, UI, CLI dedupe, memory, and LLM replay boundaries without removing the legacy sentinel fallback or asserting unrelated prompt-construction details.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The parser/contract boundary was wrong: `buildInboundUserContextPrefix(...)` creates inbound metadata as plaintext blocks for model-facing prompts, and consumers such as UI history normalization, CLI imported-session dedupe, memory-host session export, and LLM historical replay had no trusted way to distinguish host-added decoration from user-authored text in the same `content` channel.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The source-of-truth decision is no longer derived from forgeable `content` text. Consumers now use the shared trusted resolver and only accept `bareBody` when the message/context is explicitly marked `inboundDecorated: true` / `InboundDecorated: true`, so sentinel parsing remains only a legacy compatibility path.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix for a public/data-format contract bug.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High. The change is additive, keeps legacy fallback behavior, and has targeted regression coverage for the affected consumer paths.
- **Patch quality notes:** Dynamic `as unknown` casts stay at existing message-union boundaries where `AgentMessage` can carry additive metadata; guard/default returns preserve existing fallback behavior; the multi-file diff follows the shared consumer contract boundary and is not a downstream masking workaround.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The canonical contract is the inbound user-turn/message metadata (`inboundDecorated` plus `bareBody`, mirrored as `InboundDecorated` plus `BareBody` on inbound context). Display/history/CLI dedupe/memory consumers use that trusted contract when present and only parse text sentinels for unmarked legacy content.

## What Problem This Solves

Inbound context decoration is host-generated, but the decorated copy can live as text inside user-controlled user message content. Several consumers stripped or compared that decoration by matching forgeable sentinel strings such as `Sender (untrusted metadata):` and fenced JSON. If a user typed the same shape intentionally, downstream display/history/dedupe/memory paths could drop real user text or collapse distinct imported messages.

This PR gives consumers a trusted, out-of-band contract for decorated inbound turns: a message/context marker that says the content may be decorated, plus the host-authored bare body to render/replay/index/dedupe.

## Source-of-truth boundary

The canonical contract is the inbound user-turn/message metadata:

- runtime/persisted user messages: `inboundDecorated: true` plus `bareBody`
- inbound context: `InboundDecorated` plus `BareBody`

The model-facing prompt may still carry inbound context for the active turn. Display/history/CLI dedupe/memory consumers should use the trusted bare body when present, and only parse text sentinels for unmarked legacy content.

## Implementation summary

- Add optional trusted inbound fields:
  - `inboundDecorated: true` / `bareBody` on persisted/runtime user messages.
  - `InboundDecorated` / `BareBody` on inbound template context.
- Persist the trusted bare body on decorated user turns, while respecting `before_message_write` hooks as the redaction boundary: if a hook replaces content without returning trusted fields, the original `bareBody` / `inboundDecorated` is dropped.
- Update UI display, UI text extraction/export/search, CLI imported-session dedupe, memory-host session indexing, and LLM historical replay to prefer the trusted bare body before falling back to legacy sentinel stripping.
- Keep existing `stripInboundMetadata(...)` behavior for old transcripts that do not carry the trusted fields.

## Scope boundary

In scope: contract/type plumbing for inbound user turns, transcript persistence/runtime merge preservation, UI display normalization, CLI imported-session dedupe, memory-host session export/indexing, LLM historical replay normalization, and regression tests for those paths.

Out of scope: removing legacy sentinel stripping for already-persisted rows, changing active-turn model prompt construction, migrating existing transcript files, or doing a broad plugin SDK redesign beyond optional trusted fields.

## Related PR scan

The daily-fix related open PR scan found related PR #94419 as advisory background only. This PR does not depend on that work and keeps the fix scoped to the trusted inbound bare-body contract requested by #95279.

## Review findings addressed

- RF-001 / RF-004 / RF-007 / RF-008 — fixed in `src/gateway/cli-session-history.merge.ts`: the CLI imported-session comparable-text path now calls `resolveTrustedInboundBareBody(...)` for trusted decorated user messages before falling back to legacy sentinel stripping. Regression coverage: `src/gateway/cli-session-history.test.ts` verifies two imported/local user turns with identical decorated content but distinct sentinel-shaped trusted bare bodies remain two messages.
- RF-002 / RF-003 — addressed with redacted terminal proof for the CLI imported-session dedupe path. The proof called `mergeImportedChatHistoryMessages(...)` with two trusted bare bodies and identical decorated content; output showed `mergedCount: 2`, `preservedDistinctTrustedBareBodies: true`, and `wouldHaveCollidedByDecoratedContent: true`.
- RF-005 / RF-006 — addressed by keeping the durable contract explicit and additive: `inboundDecorated` gates trust in `bareBody`, consumers use the shared resolver, legacy unmarked rows keep sentinel fallback behavior, and `before_message_write` remains the redaction boundary by dropping original trusted fields when hooks replace content without returning trusted fields themselves.
- RF-009 — passed: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/cli-session-history.test.ts` completed successfully with no test failures.
- RF-010 — passed: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/inbound.test.ts src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.test.ts` completed successfully with no test failures, and the same run was repeated including `src/gateway/cli-session-history.test.ts` after the CLI dedupe fix was committed.
- RF-011 — passed: `git diff --check` completed with exit code 0 and no whitespace errors.
- Previous ClawSweeper P1 (`before_message_write` redaction boundary) — fixed in `src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.ts`: `preparePersistedUserTurnMessageForTranscriptWrite(...)` now preserves the original idempotency key only. If `before_message_write` replaces/redacts a user message and does not return trusted inbound fields itself, the original `bareBody` and `inboundDecorated` are not reattached. Regression coverage: `src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.test.ts` extends the existing redaction/idempotency test with a decorated secret prompt and asserts the persisted redacted row has no original trusted bare-body fields.
- Previous ClawSweeper P1 (`BareBody` sanitizer) — fixed in `src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-context.ts`: `BareBody` now uses the same inbound system-marker neutralization path as `RawBody` / `CommandBody` instead of newline-only trusted normalization. Regression coverage: `src/auto-reply/inbound.test.ts` asserts `[System Message]` and line-leading `System:` markers are neutralized on `BareBody`.
- Previous ClawSweeper UI extraction finding — fixed in `ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.ts`: `extractText(...)` / `extractTextCached(...)` now use the shared `resolveTrustedInboundBareBody(...)` path for trusted decorated user messages before legacy sentinel stripping. Regression coverage: `ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.test.ts` verifies sentinel-shaped user-authored text is preserved for export/search/text-extraction consumers.

## Verification

Passed locally:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/cli-session-history.test.ts src/auto-reply/inbound.test.ts src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.test.ts
```

```text
git diff --check
```

## Merge risk

- Risk labels considered: `message-delivery`, `security-boundary`, `session-state`.
- Risk explanation: This changes display/replay/indexing/dedupe behavior for messages that explicitly opt into the trusted decorated contract. The security-boundary risk is that consumers now trust `bareBody`; it is gated by `inboundDecorated === true`, populated by host-side preparation/persistence paths, and not restored across hook redaction unless the hook returns trusted fields itself. Session-state risk is additive because existing transcripts without the marker keep the old sentinel fallback.
- Why acceptable: The contract is optional and backward-compatible. It narrows unsafe behavior by making trusted fields authoritative only when present, while retaining legacy stripping for old rows. The tests cover the paths most likely to drop or dedupe user-authored content.

target.title: fix(exec): stop forcing approval for safe commands with harmless redirections
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

In `security: "allowlist"` mode, OpenClaw treats any command containing a shell redirection as an allowlist miss and forces a manual approval prompt — even when every command segment is already allowlisted/safe and the redirection only discards or merges a standard stream. Routine safe commands like `ls 2>/dev/null` or `grep … 2>&1 | head` interrupt the operator with an approval prompt despite touching no filesystem.

## Why This Change Was Made

Stream-only redirections — `>/dev/null`, `1>/dev/null`, `2>/dev/null`, `2>&1`, `1>&2` — are now classified as harmless and excluded from the unanalyzable-risk set in the POSIX authorization planner (`src/infra/exec-authorization-plan.ts`), so a command whose redirections are all harmless falls through to the existing per-segment allowlist/safeBins evaluation. Redirections never alter the parsed argv, so the same program-safety check still applies. Anything that writes or reads a real file (`> file`, `>> file`, `2> err.log`, `< in`), uses `&>`, closes/duplicates to an arbitrary fd (`>&-`, `2>&3`), or targets anything other than `/dev/null` or fd 1/2 still blocks. Non-goals: no change to which programs are considered safe, and no new config surface.

## User Impact

Operators running in allowlist mode no longer get approval prompts for safe commands that only redirect to `/dev/null` or merge stdout/stderr. The security posture is unchanged for any redirection that touches the filesystem — those still require approval exactly as before.

## Evidence

Developed AI-assisted (Claude Code) and gated by a Codex review pass on **both the implementation plan and the final diff**; all Codex findings were addressed (regex narrowed to fd 1/2; the end-to-end test made non-vacuous via `analysisOk`; adversarial bypass cases added).

**Scope:** 5 files — the classifier in `src/infra/exec-authorization-plan.ts`, its two colocated test files, and two public docs pages (`docs/tools/exec.md`, `docs/tools/exec-approvals-advanced.md`) updated to match the new behavior. No config, no `CHANGELOG`.

**Unit + integration tests** (`src/infra/exec-authorization-plan.test.ts` = plan-layer classification incl. an `adversarial redirection bypass attempts` block; `src/infra/exec-authorization-allowlist.test.ts` = end-to-end through `evaluateShellAllowlistWithAuthorization` + `requiresExecApproval`):

```
$ pnpm test src/infra/exec-authorization-plan.test.ts src/infra/exec-authorization-allowlist.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  61 passed (61)
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)
```

Adjacent suites, no regressions (`exec-approvals-policy`, `exec-allow-always-persistence`, `exec-approvals-allow-always`, `exec-approvals-safe-bins`, `exec-approvals-parity`, `command-explainer/extract`): **228 passed**.

**Security + behavioral proof against the compiled `dist` bundle.** This harness imports the *bundled* `planShellAuthorization` / `evaluateShellAllowlistWithAuthorization` from `dist/` (production build, not the test transform) and runs the real commands. The `plan.ok=false` rows are disguised redirects that **stay blocked**; the end-to-end rows show the real approval decision (`head` is a default safe bin):

```
== plan-layer (compiled dist) ==
PASS  plan.ok=true  exp=true   ls 2>/dev/null
PASS  plan.ok=true  exp=true   ls >/dev/null
PASS  plan.ok=true  exp=true   ls 1>/dev/null
PASS  plan.ok=true  exp=true   cat 2>&1
PASS  plan.ok=true  exp=true   cat 1>&2
PASS  plan.ok=true  exp=true   ls 2>/dev/null 1>/dev/null
PASS  plan.ok=true  exp=true   ls 2> /dev/null
PASS  plan.ok=true  exp=true   echo test 2>/dev/null | grep -e "test" | head -1
PASS  plan.ok=false exp=false  ls > out.txt
PASS  plan.ok=false exp=false  ls 2>error.log
PASS  plan.ok=false exp=false  cat < input.txt
PASS  plan.ok=false exp=false  grep -e "pattern" 2>/dev/null > /tmp/results.txt
PASS  plan.ok=false exp=false  ls >>/dev/null
PASS  plan.ok=false exp=false  ls &>/dev/null
PASS  plan.ok=false exp=false  head >/dev/null/../../../etc/crontab
PASS  plan.ok=false exp=false  head 2>&3
PASS  plan.ok=false exp=false  head 3>/dev/null
PASS  plan.ok=false exp=false  head >| /dev/null
PASS  plan.ok=false exp=false  head >"/dev/null"
PASS  plan.ok=false exp=false  $(whoami) 2>/dev/null

== end-to-end approval (compiled dist), head is a default safe bin ==
PASS  prompt=false exp=false (analysisOk=true, allowlistSatisfied=true)  head -n 1 2>/dev/null
PASS  prompt=true  exp=true  (analysisOk=false, allowlistSatisfied=false)  head -n 1 > /tmp/x
PASS  prompt=true  exp=true  (analysisOk=true, allowlistSatisfied=false)  head -n 1 2>/dev/null; rm -rf /tmp/x

RESULT: 23 passed, 0 failed
```

**Other gates:** `pnpm build` green; `pnpm tsgo` + `pnpm check:test-types` clean; `oxfmt` and `oxlint` clean. The full repo-wide suite runs in CI on this PR.

### AI-assisted PR checklist
- [x] Marked as AI-assisted (Claude Code; Codex review gate on plan + diff)
- [x] Concise Evidence section with verbatim validation output
- [x] Author understands what the code does
- [x] Codex review run and findings addressed before opening
- [x] Will resolve/reply to bot review conversations as they arrive

target.title: fix(slack): allow channel-id reads for name-allowlisted channels
target.body: ## What Problem This Solves Slack action tools can target a channel by Slack channel ID, for example when reading a permalink or operating on a message outside the current thread. If an operator configured Slack channel access by human-readable channel name (for example `#allowed-channel`) with `dangerouslyAllowNameMatching` enabled, those channel-ID reads were rejected before the name allowlist could be applied. That blocked legitimate read-like Slack actions such as reading messages, downloading files, listing pins, and listing reactions for channels that were configured as allowed by name. ## How This Changes Behavior - Lazily resolves Slack conversation names through Slack `conversations.info` when an action has only a channel ID and the account explicitly enabled name matching. - Re-checks read authorization with that trusted Slack API room name and still fails closed if no allowed name is found. - Does **not** trust generic threading context fields such as `Channel` as Slack room names for authorization. - Removes the `currentChannelName` threading/action shortcut so read authorization cannot be satisfied by an untrusted context-provided name. - Reuses the existing conversation info cache and returns names from that helper. ## Security / Authorization Notes This PR keeps channel-name authorization behind the existing `dangerouslyAllowNameMatching` opt-in. Name-based authorization only happens after resolving the target Slack channel ID through Slack's API. A core-shaped tool context like `Channel: "slack"` is treated as provider metadata, not as a Slack room name, and cannot authorize a `#slack` allowlist entry. ## Evidence - `pnpm test:extension slack` — 101 files / 1336 tests passed. - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/slack/src/threading-tool-context.test.ts extensions/slack/src/channel-type.test.ts extensions/slack/src/message-action-dispatch.test.ts` — 4 files / 121 tests passed. - `pnpm tsgo:extensions` — passed. - `git diff --check` — passed. - Added synthetic-fixture coverage for: - resolving a name-allowlisted read from a real core-shaped Slack threading context; - rejecting provider metadata (`Channel: "slack"`) as a Slack room-name authorization source; - rejecting a different target channel even when the current context channel ID is allowed; - resolving a channel name via `conversations.info` before failing an allowlist read; - resolving a name-keyed allow before applying wildcard fallback denial; - returning conversation names from the Slack channel-type helper. Not sure if this is acceptable proof, but in the past month, I had multiple OpenClaws on different machines collaborate with one another via a shared whitelisted Slack channel. Recently (as in the past ~3-5 days), after I upgraded to 2026.6.8, the ability for me to have bot-to-bot conversations went away. My use case was Bot A does dev work on a repo; Bot B does deployment of a said PR to servers C it has access to. A does not have access to C, but can do so indirectly through B. Example of it working from the past month: Example of it NOT working: ## Privacy / Fixture Notes All examples in this PR use fabricated Slack fixtures such as `#allowed-channel`, `C0123456789`, and `C9876543210`; no private workspace channel names, channel IDs, user IDs, or message timestamps are included. ## Redacted Runtime Proof I installed and ran this branch from source on a local OpenClaw gateway, then exercised the Slack `message.read` path against a live Slack account. Real workspace/channel/user identifiers are intentionally replaced here with fabricated fixtures. ```text OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (ee83402) branch: fix/slack-read-allowlist-channel-name-public ``` Relevant redacted config shape: ```json5 { channels: { slack: { groupPolicy: "allowlist", dangerouslyAllowNameMatching: true, channels: { "#allowed-channel": { enabled: true } } } } } ``` Allowed-channel read by Slack channel ID: ```text action: message.read target: channel:C0123456789 threadId: 1234567890.123456 result: ok=true result.messages.length: 1 result.hasMore: true ``` Denied-channel read by Slack channel ID: ```text action: message.read target: channel:C9876543210 result.status: error result.error: Slack read target channel is not allowed. ``` This verifies the patched runtime accepts a Slack read where the action target is a Slack channel ID while the allowlist entry is a channel name resolved at runtime, and still fails closed for non-matching channel IDs.

commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a Telegram polling-session recovery path that fails timed-out live-owned spooled ingress claims before draining later same-lane updates, plus a regression test.

PR surface: Source +41, Tests +44. Total +85 across 2 files.

target.title: [codex] fix Telegram live-owned stuck ingress claims
target.body:
Summary

- Fail timed-out Telegram spooled ingress claims that are still owned by a live process but no longer have active handler state in this gateway.
- Keep deferred claims and active in-memory lanes guarded so truly active work is not released.
- Add regression coverage proving a wedged live-owned same-lane claim is failed and the later update drains without replaying the stuck update.

Root cause

The Telegram isolated ingress drain recovered dead or unowned durable claims, but it skipped any claim held by a live process. If that live worker stopped making progress without leaving active handler state behind, the durable `channel_ingress_events` row stayed claimed and later same-lane updates remained blocked until gateway restart or manual SQL cleanup.

Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/message/ingress-queue.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Fixes #95248

target.title: fix(telegram): detect EADDRNOTAVAIL and skip misleading IP-rotation fallback
target.body:
## Summary

When Telegram's Bot API returns a `ECONNREFUSED`, `EADDRNOTAVAIL`, or `EAI_AGAIN` error during getUpdates (typically due to IP rotation or DNS propagation delays after a bot token reset), OpenClaw was logging a misleading "IP rotation detected" message that suggests a config change is needed. In reality, the connection error is transient and self-resolving.

This fix detects specific socket-level errors (EADDRNOTAVAIL, ECONNREFUSED, EAI_AGAIN) during Telegram API calls and logs an actionable message instead of the misleading IP-rotation fallback message.

Fixes #94620

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Socket-level errors (EADDRNOTAVAIL, ECONNREFUSED, EAI_AGAIN) during Telegram API calls no longer trigger the misleading "IP rotation detected" message.

**Real environment tested**: Linux 6.8.0-124-generic / Node.js v25.9.0 / OpenClaw latest main built with gatewayWatch profile / Live Telegram bot (@lizeyu_openclaw_bot)

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
# Build and start gateway with Telegram
node scripts/build-all.mjs gatewayWatch
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/.openclaw-telegram OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=~/.openclaw/openclaw.json \
  node openclaw.mjs gateway --port 18789 --verbose

# Run all fetch tests
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/fetch.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

**After-fix evidence**: Real console output from gateway startup and tests:
```
$ curl -s http://127.0.0.1:18789/healthz
{"ok":true,"status":"live"}

Gateway logs:
[channels/telegram] [default] starting provider (@lizeyu_openclaw_bot)
[telegram][diag] isolated polling ingress started
[routing] resolveAgentRoute: channel=telegram accountId=default

No "IP rotation detected" misleading message — clean startup.

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/fetch.test.ts
✓ extensions/telegram/src/fetch.test.ts (tests pass)
```

**Observed result after the fix**: The Telegram transport starts cleanly without emitting the misleading IP-rotation fallback message. Socket errors are now detected by their error code and logged with an appropriate actionable message.

**What was not tested**: Live EADDRNOTAVAIL scenario (requires a real network condition where the error occurs). The error classification is verified through unit tests.

## Tests and validation

```
✓ extensions/telegram/src/fetch.test.ts (43 tests)
  - does not retry fallback on EADDRNOTAVAIL and logs diagnostic
  - does not retry when error codes do not match fallback rules
  - retries sticky fallback when the local network is down during connect
  - keeps per-resolver transport policy isolated across multiple accounts
  - transport lifecycle: close, idempotent close, destroy failure swallowed
✓ extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts (59 tests)
```

## Risk checklist

- [x] This change is backwards compatible
- [x] This change has been tested with existing configurations
- [x] I have updated relevant documentation
- [x] Breaking changes (if any) are documented in Summary

## Current review state

- [x] Self-review completed
- [ ] At least one maintainer review
- [ ] All CI checks passed

target.title: [AI-assisted] fix(memory): close cached agent and state databases in test teardowns
target.body:
This PR resolves Windows file locking (EBUSY/EPERM) errors during test cleanup in the memory-core extension.

### Cause
When tests instantiate MemoryIndexManager using the default backend 'builtin', they register the agent database path which opens and caches the state database (openclaw.sqlite) connection via openOpenClawStateDatabase / registerAgentDatabase. The state database connection is cached globally in openclaw-state-db.ts's cachedDatabases and is never closed during the test's afterEach or afterAll hooks. On Windows, this open file handle locks the state database file, blocking the test suite from unlinking the temporary fixtureRoot directories.

### Fix
Import and invoke closeOpenClawAgentDatabasesForTest() and closeOpenClawStateDatabaseForTest() from 'openclaw/plugin-sdk/sqlite-runtime-testing' in afterEach and afterAll hooks of the affected memory-core test files to ensure all open SQLite database handles are fully closed before unlinking test directories.

Closes #78640

Real behavior proof: Ran vitest run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.reindex-recovery.test.ts and vitest run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.watcher-config.test.ts on a Windows host and confirmed all tests pass cleanly without EBUSY errors.

target.title: fix(googlechat): sanitize internal tool-trace lines from outbound text (#90684)
target.body:
## Summary

- Google Chat outbound text only ran `sanitizeForPlainText()`, so internal assistant trace lines leaked to users — most visibly the `⚠️ 🛠️ \`<command> (agent)\` failed` banner produced for a benign non-zero shell exit (e.g. a `grep` that finds no match → exit 1). The turn completes successfully, but the user gets an alarming "failed" message.
- Fix: run `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText()` before `sanitizeForPlainText()` in the googlechat outbound `sanitizeText` hook — the same composition Discord (`sanitizeDiscordFrontChannelText`) and WhatsApp (`normalizeWhatsAppPayloadText`) already use. The hook runs pre-chunking in the shared deliver path, so it covers every text send (replies, sub-agent announces, cron deliveries).
- Out of scope: this does NOT close the broader cross-channel umbrella (#90684) for Telegram/Feishu/Slack/msteams, and it does not change the Codex-native warning taxonomy (#88332). It is one focused Google Chat instance.
- Success: a benign non-zero shell exit no longer emits a user-visible `⚠️ 🛠️ … failed` banner in Google Chat; ordinary prose is untouched.

## Linked context

Closes part of #90684 (Google Chat instance of the non-Discord assistant-visible sanitizer gap).
Related #88332, #87610.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Google Chat leaked the internal `⚠️ 🛠️ \`<command> (agent)\` failed` trace banner (and similar trace lines) to end users because outbound text skipped `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText()`. Reproduced live: a front-desk agent answered a user correctly, but the channel also posted a standalone bot message `⚠️ 🛠️ search "…" in ~/.openclaw/workspace-* (agent) failed` from a `grep` that exited 1 (no match).
- Real environment tested: Production OpenClaw `2026.6.8` gateway running the installed `@openclaw/googlechat` plugin with this patch applied to the live adapter, on a real Google Chat deployment. `/health` returned `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: In the deployed plugin's own module context, ran `node` to exercise the patched outbound `sanitizeText` composition on the exact leaked banner and on ordinary prose, then scanned trajectories captured after the patch went live for the trigger condition (benign non-zero tool exits):

```
# 1) deployed outbound sanitize composition (exactly what the patched hook runs)
node -e 'import("openclaw/plugin-sdk/text-chunking").then(async tc => {
  const { sanitizeForPlainText } = await import("openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-outbound");
  const hook = t => sanitizeForPlainText(tc.sanitizeAssistantVisibleText(t));
  console.log("OUT:", JSON.stringify(hook(
    "Listo.\n⚠️ 🛠️ `search \"Pipeline\" in ~/.openclaw/workspace-* (agent)` failed")));
  console.log("normal:", JSON.stringify(hook("El pipeline tiene 3 deals.")));
})'

# 2) did the trigger condition actually occur in live traffic since deploy?
node scan-trajectories.mjs   # counts tool failures / exit-1 captured after deploy
```

- Evidence after fix: copied live output / console output (stdout) from the patched `2026.6.8` deployment, captured with `node` against the live plugin module:

```
OUT: "Listo."
normal: "El pipeline tiene 3 deals."

trigger-events (tool fail / exit 1) after deploy: 11  (across 2 agents)
banner present in delivered/outbound payload after deploy: 0
```

- Observed result after fix: the patched `node` hook removes the `⚠️ 🛠️ … (agent) failed` banner line and returns only the real assistant prose (`"Listo."`); ordinary text passes through unchanged. Over ~16h of live traffic the benign-exit trigger fired 11 times and zero failed-tool banners reached the channel. No recurrence reported by the user who originally hit it.
- What was not tested: I did not capture a screenshot of the Google Chat client UI itself (proof is copied live `node`/stdout output from the patched deployment, not a client screen recording). I did not exercise the broader Telegram/Feishu/Slack/msteams paths (out of scope; tracked by #90684).
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: importing the full `channel.adapters` ESM module standalone fails outside the gateway process (runtime-context deps), so the after-fix proof exercises the exact same two-function composition the patched hook runs (`sanitizeForPlainText(sanitizeAssistantVisibleText(text))`) resolved from the live plugin's own module context, rather than re-instantiating the whole adapter object. Space IDs / account IDs / user details are intentionally omitted.
- Before evidence: pre-patch, the identical banner reached the user as a standalone bot message (`⚠️ 🛠️ search "…" in ~/.openclaw/workspace-* (agent) failed`) while the actual answer was delivered fine; on the same runtime `node` shows `sanitizeForPlainText(text)` alone returns the banner unchanged, whereas `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText(text)` strips it.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm vitest run extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts -t "sanitizeText"` → 2 passed (new `googlechatPlugin outbound sanitizeText` describe block): asserts the `(agent) failed` banner is stripped via the real `googlechatOutboundAdapter.base.sanitizeText` hook, and that ordinary prose is preserved.
- Regression coverage added in `channel.test.ts`. CI/unit tests are supplemental; the after-fix proof above is copied live output from a real deployment.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? Yes — internal trace/failure banners are no longer shown in Google Chat; legitimate prose is unaffected.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No — this narrows user-visible internal-trace leakage only; no deps/workflows/credentials/permissions touched.
- Highest-risk area: over-stripping legitimate content. Mitigated by reusing the canonical `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText()` (already used by Discord/WhatsApp) rather than new regexes, plus the new unit test asserting ordinary prose is preserved.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review. Structured after-fix real-behavior proof from a live patched deployment added above (addressing the ClawSweeper proof blocker).
- Waiting on: maintainer review. The unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents-core-tools` shard failure does not touch `extensions/googlechat`.
- Bot comments addressed: ClawSweeper "needs real behavior proof before merge" — added structured after-fix evidence and observed result from a real Google Chat deployment.

AI-assisted change.

target.title: fix: keep text transform runtime imports hashed
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes a released-package gateway crash-loop path where new chunks can import expanded text-transform exports through a stable alias that an already-running gateway may have cached from an older package.
- Keeps new `text-transforms.runtime` imports on the hashed runtime chunk while still generating the stable wrapper for old chunks.
- Adds legacy compat wrappers for the shipped v2026.5.22 and v2026.6.8 text-transform runtime hashes.
- Out of scope: changing provider runtime behavior or plugin text-transform semantics.

## Linked context

Closes #95057

Related #95057

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Requested in maintainer workflow to repair the P1 release regression.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: macOS LaunchAgent-managed gateway can crash-loop after upgrading when new provider/CLI chunks import `n/i/a/r/t` from `./text-transforms.runtime.js` but the live process has an older stable wrapper cached that only exposed `t`.
- Real environment tested: local source checkout on macOS with Node 22 toolchain and npm package inspection of published `openclaw@2026.5.22` and `openclaw@2026.6.8`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs` after a source build had produced `dist`; then inspected generated importers with `rg -n "text-transforms\.runtime\.js|text-transforms\.runtime-[A-Za-z0-9_-]+\.js" dist/provider-runtime*.js dist/cli-backends*.js dist/prepare.runtime*.js dist/execute.runtime*.js dist/selection*.js dist/compact*.js` and inspected `dist/text-transforms.runtime-D9-SpAmI.js` plus `dist/text-transforms.runtime-sEqsN4pN.js`.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
--- runtime-postbuild stderr ---
runtime-postbuild: stable root runtime imports completed in 5858ms
runtime-postbuild: stable root runtime aliases completed in 572ms
runtime-postbuild: legacy root runtime compat aliases completed in 1ms
--- importers ---
dist/cli-backends-uScbGHoe.js:4:import { r as mergePluginTextTransforms, t as resolveRuntimeTextTransforms } from "./text-transforms.runtime-DhZfO7FJ.js";
dist/provider-runtime-Cqktwbuy.js:10:import { n as applyPluginTextReplacements, r as mergePluginTextTransforms, t as resolveRuntimeTextTransforms } from "./text-transforms.runtime-DhZfO7FJ.js";
dist/compact-pRQItHot.js:12:import { i as wrapStreamFnTextTransforms } from "./text-transforms.runtime-DhZfO7FJ.js";
dist/execute.runtime-DCIHvX5t.js:5:import { n as applyPluginTextReplacements } from "./text-transforms.runtime-DhZfO7FJ.js";
dist/prepare.runtime-Db3n21Sv.js:11:import { n as applyPluginTextReplacements } from "./text-transforms.runtime-DhZfO7FJ.js";
dist/selection-DS_psmoB.js:34:import { a as createStreamIteratorWrapper, i as wrapStreamFnTextTransforms } from "./text-transforms.runtime-DhZfO7FJ.js";
--- compat ---
export * from "./text-transforms.runtime.js";
export * from "./text-transforms.runtime.js";
```

- Observed result after fix: new chunks no longer depend on the stable `text-transforms.runtime.js` module namespace for expanded exports, while old shipped hashed runtime names still resolve through compat wrappers.
- What was not tested: a full macOS Apple Silicon LaunchAgent upgrade from `openclaw@2026.5.22` to this unpublished branch package.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: `pnpm build` completed `tsdown` and `runtime-postbuild` but timed out at 15 minutes during the later plugin SDK d.ts stage, so the package-level proof is based on the completed generated `dist` runtime-postbuild output plus focused tests rather than a full build command exit 0.
- Before evidence:

```text
2026.5.22 dist/text-transforms.runtime.js:
export * from "./text-transforms.runtime-D9-SpAmI.js";

2026.5.22 dist/text-transforms.runtime-D9-SpAmI.js export surface:
export { resolveRuntimeTextTransforms as t };

2026.6.8 importer examples:
dist/provider-runtime-Cp9BfFXg.js imports n/r/t from "./text-transforms.runtime.js"
dist/cli-backends-f9hsjoJk.js imports r/t from "./text-transforms.runtime.js"
dist/selection-kQiC501t.js imports a/i from "./text-transforms.runtime.js"
```

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/runtime-postbuild.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs test/scripts/runtime-postbuild.test.ts`
- `node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added runtime-postbuild tests proving text-transform runtime importers stay hashed after the stable alias export surface expansion.
- Added runtime-postbuild tests proving previous published text-transform runtime hash names get compat wrappers.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The package comparison shows the old stable wrapper exposed only `t`, while the new released chunks import more aliases through the same stable module path. That can fail in a live process with a cached older wrapper namespace.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- Yes. It changes generated package runtime import paths to avoid upgrade-time gateway crashes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Runtime package postbuild aliasing for live upgrades.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The change is scoped to one runtime alias skip and two shipped hash compat entries, with focused fixture tests and generated `dist` proof that old compat wrappers and new hashed importers coexist.

target.title: fix(ui): change silent cron quick-create sessionTarget to isolated
target.body:
## Summary

The "Silent" delivery preset in the Cron quick-create dialog (`cron-quick-create.ts`) maps to `sessionTarget: "main"`, but the server-side validation in `assertSupportedJobSpec()` rejects `sessionTarget="main"` combined with `payloadKind="agentTurn"` (only `"systemEvent"` payloads are allowed in main sessions). Since the wizard always uses `agentTurn` payloads, the "Silent" option silently fails — `addCronJob` throws on the server, the error is swallowed by `withCronBusy`, and the dialog remains open with no visible feedback to the user.

The fix changes `sessionTarget` from `"main"` to `"isolated"` for the "Silent" preset. The `deliveryMode: "none"` already ensures no result is announced, preserving the silent semantics. The "Notify me" and "Independent session" presets are unaffected because they already use `sessionTarget: "isolated"`.

Fixes #95073

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Silent cron quick-create preset now successfully creates jobs instead of failing silently due to server-side sessionTarget validation

**Real environment tested**: Ubuntu 24.04, Node.js v24, pnpm 11.2.2, OpenClaw 2026.6.1 (upstream main aa479ac7d8)

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/cron-quick-create.node.test.ts
pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/
```

**After-fix evidence**:
```
$ pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/cron-quick-create.node.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)   ← new test for silent preset passes

$ pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/
 Test Files  29 passed (29)
      Tests  297 passed (297)   ← all view tests pass, no regression
```

**Observed result after the fix**: The "Silent" preset now maps to `sessionTarget: "isolated"` which the server accepts with `payloadKind: "agentTurn"`. All 297 view tests pass with no regression.

**What was not tested**: Full Gateway integration test (the fix is a client-side mapping change; server validation already covers the isolated + agentTurn combination for all existing presets).

## Tests and validation

### Unit tests

```
$ pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/cron-quick-create.node.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)
   Start at  06:13:24
   Duration  418ms

$ pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/
 Test Files  29 passed (29)
      Tests  297 passed (297)
   Start at  06:13:32
   Duration  17.89s
```

### Root cause

1. `draftToCronFormPatch()` in `cron-quick-create.ts` maps "silent" → `sessionTarget: "main"`, `deliveryMode: "none"`
2. The mapped form state is merged with `DEFAULT_CRON_FORM` which has `payloadKind: "agentTurn"`
3. `addCronJob()` sends the job to the server via `client.request("cron.add", job)`
4. Server `assertSupportedJobSpec()` in `jobs.ts:286` throws: `'main cron jobs require payload.kind="systemEvent"'`
5. The error is caught by `withCronBusy()` → `state.cronError` is set, `saved = false`
6. The dialog stays open because `saved` is false — no visible feedback

The fix changes line 192 from `"main"` to `"isolated"`, matching what the server accepts.

## Risk checklist

- [x] This change is backwards compatible — older cron jobs are unaffected; only the quick-create mapping for the "Silent" option changes
- [x] This change has been tested with existing configurations — all 297 view tests pass
- [ ] I have updated relevant documentation — N/A, the fix is a one-line mapping correction
- [x] Breaking changes (if any) are documented in Summary — no breaking changes

## Current review state

- [x] Self-review completed
- [ ] At least one maintainer review
- [ ] All CI checks passed

target.title: fix(embedded-agent): auto-recover provider-rejected image history (closes #94906)
target.body:
## Summary

When a provider (e.g. MiniMax-M3 1026 "image is sensitive") rejects a recent image tool result as sensitive, the offending image block was already persisted into the session transcript. The existing `pruneProcessedHistoryImages()` only prunes images after several successful assistant turns, so every retry replayed the same rejected image and wedged the session. Operators had to `/reset` or hand-edit the transcript to recover.

This change adds a narrow recovery path and surfaces an actionable error message to chat users.

## Changes

- **`src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/image-rejection-error.ts` (new)**: narrow `isSensitiveImageRejectionError` detector matching `image is sensitive` / `new_sensitive` / `messages[N].content[M]` patterns, plus `formatSensitiveImageRejectionErrorCopy` for the user-facing string.
- **`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/image-rejection-recovery.ts` (new)**: planner + recovery that walks the most recent toolResult/user branch, replaces image blocks with a text placeholder while keeping text context, appends an `openclaw:image-rejection-recovery` custom entry, and rebuilds the in-memory session messages.
- **`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/image-rejection-recovery.test.ts` (new)**: 6 cases covering detector narrowness, planner targeting only the most recent toolResult/user image, turn-by-turn precision (older images untouched, placeholder retained across recoveries, new images untouched), and the documented multi-image toolResult limitation.
- **`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`**: invoke the recovery in the promptError path, and also when the terminal assistant `errorMessage` carries the sensitive-image pattern even without an outer promptError.
- **`src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.ts`**: route the actionable copy through `sanitizeUserFacingText` for last-mile sanitization of `isError` payloads.
- **`src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.ts`**: in `formatUserFacingAssistantErrorText`, check the raw provider `errorMessage` for the sensitive-image pattern **before** the `isRawAssistantErrorPassthrough` check, so the rich error isn't dropped into `GENERIC_ASSISTANT_ERROR_TEXT` ("LLM request failed.") just because the parsed friendly text starts with "LLM error api_error: ".

## User-visible result

Before this change, a rejected image gave a wedged session and the user only saw a generic "LLM request failed." with no signal that the image was the cause.

After this change, the user sees:

> LLM request failed: provider rejected a recent image block as sensitive. OpenClaw removed the image data from session history while keeping text context; please retry or continue.

The next turn works (the image block has been replaced with a text placeholder in the active branch), so a manual `/reset` is no longer required.

## Known limitations

- Multi-image toolResult (a single read returning N images in its content array) has every image stripped, not just the rejected one. The most common case is single-image reads, which is correctly targeted. This trade-off is documented in `image-rejection-recovery.test.ts`.
- The error text is exposed to the user (not silently auto-retried), so the user is always told when an image was rejected.

## Test plan

- `pnpm test --run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/image-rejection-recovery.test.ts` — 6/6 pass
- `pnpm test --run src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.formatassistanterrortext.test.ts` — 82/82 pass
- `pnpm test --run src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers` — 55/55 pass
- Manually reproduced end-to-end against MiniMax-M3 on a Feishu session: recovery log fires, next turn succeeds, actionable message is delivered to the chat.

Closes #94906.

target.title: fix(ui): restore in-flight streaming assistant reply on Control UI session switch-back
target.body:
## Summary

When you switch away from a Control UI chat session while the agent is actively generating a response and then switch back, the in-progress streaming reply was lost — only the last user message was shown.

The gateway already exposes the still-streaming run via `chat.history` / `chat.startup` responses (the `inFlightRun` field), and the TUI already uses this to restore state on reconnect. The Control UI was simply ignoring it.

## Root cause

Three things were missing in `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts`:

1. **`ChatHistoryResult` type** didn't include `inFlightRun` — the field was silently dropped
2. **No adoption logic** — the history loader ignored the gateway's in-flight run data
3. **The maintainer's concern from previous PRs**: `inFlightRun.text` is the cumulative gateway buffer that overlaps with already-persisted assistant messages; without reconciliation, the UI would show duplicate text

Three previous PRs attempted this fix (#90832, #92969, #93273) but all were closed — two because they lacked the text reconciliation step.

## Fix

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts` | Add `inFlightRun` to `ChatHistoryResult` type |
| `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts` | Create `adoptInFlightRunFromChatHistory()` with text overlap reconciliation |
| `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts` | Call adoption after stream reconciliation when no fresher local run exists |
| `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts` | Add 5 tests covering: basic adoption, empty-text adoption, overlap reconciliation, missing runId, and local-run preservation |

### Reconciliation logic

```
inFlightRun.text: "Silent pond,\nA frog jumps in—\nSplash!"
last committed assistant: "Silent pond,"
→ Adopted stream text: "\nA frog jumps in—\nSplash!"
```

This prevents duplicating already-persisted text while preserving the new suffix.

Fixes #90755

## Tests and validation

- ✅ Restores buffered in-flight assistant stream on switch-back
- ✅ Adopts empty-text run for reading-indicator state
- ✅ Reconciles overlapping text with last committed assistant message
- ✅ Ignores inFlightRun without a run id
- ✅ Does not clobber a fresher local run that survived stream reconciliation

## Risk checklist

**Did user-visible behavior change?** Yes — the Control UI now restores the streaming assistant reply on session switch-back instead of showing an empty/idle thread.

**Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** No.

**Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** No.

**What is the highest-risk area?** Low — the `adoptInFlightRunFromChatHistory` function only activates when `resetStream && !state.chatRunId` (no live local run exists), so it cannot clobber an active streaming session. The text reconciliation prevents duplicate rendering.

target.title: ci: gate PR edit automation by changed field
target.body:
Goal: reduce strain on resources by optimizing which PR edits trigger which automations.

## Summary
- gate PR `edited` automation on the fields each workflow actually consumes
- keep ClawSweeper dispatch active for title/body/base edits because it serializes PR title into its activity payload
- skip expensive PR automation for metadata-only edits while preserving title/body/base behavior
- add workflow guard coverage and changed-test routing for the edited-field matrix

## What gets optimized
The optimization is: when a PR is edited, each automation wakes only when that edited field can change the job output.

- `Auto response` (`auto-response`): wakes for title, body, or base edits.
- `ClawSweeper Dispatch` (`dispatch`): wakes for title, body, or base edits.
- `Labeler` (`label`): wakes for title or base edits.
- `Real behavior proof` (`Real behavior proof`): wakes for body or base edits.

Metadata-only edits, such as milestone/assignee/project changes, skip these PR edited-event jobs because none of them read those fields.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts`
- `node scripts/check-workflows.mjs .github/workflows/auto-response.yml .github/workflows/clawsweeper-dispatch.yml .github/workflows/labeler.yml .github/workflows/real-behavior-proof.yml`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts"`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: PR `pull_request_target` `edited` automation now wakes only for fields each workflow reads.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on branch `optimize-pr-edit-automation` at commit `b480e41f2f3`, plus GitHub PR observer/check-rollup inspection for PR #94682.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts
node scripts/check-workflows.mjs .github/workflows/auto-response.yml .github/workflows/clawsweeper-dispatch.yml .github/workflows/labeler.yml .github/workflows/real-behavior-proof.yml
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts"
```

Evidence after fix:
```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  189 passed (189)
zizmor...................................................................Passed
No direct inputs interpolation found in composite run blocks.
autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
```

PR observer outcome matrix from the proposed branch workflow guards:

| PR event | auto-response | dispatch | label | Real behavior proof |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| opened | success | success | success | success |
| title edit | success | success | success | skipped |
| body edit | success | success | skipped | success |
| base edit | success | success | success | success |
| metadata edit | skipped | skipped | skipped | skipped |

Observed result after fix: skipped outcomes are intentional. Title edits do not affect real-behavior proof content, body edits do not affect labels, and metadata edits do not affect any of these automation outputs. Base edits wake all four jobs because base can affect routing, labels, proof context, and dispatch context.

Labeler edited-step outcomes:

| PR edit | `actions/labeler` | `Apply PR size label` | `Apply maintainer or trusted-contributor label` | `Apply beta-blocker title label` | `Apply too-many-prs label` |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| title edit | skipped | skipped | skipped | success | skipped |
| base edit | success | success | skipped | skipped | skipped |
| body edit | skipped | skipped | skipped | skipped | skipped |
| metadata edit | skipped | skipped | skipped | skipped | skipped |

Live PR observer/check-rollup note: current PR head previously showed `Auto response`, `ClawSweeper Dispatch`, `Labeler`, and `Real behavior proof` succeeding on PR #94682. GitHub evaluates `pull_request_target` workflow definitions from the target branch, so live PR runs cannot authoritatively execute this branch's new guards before merge. The matrix above is accepted synthetic/static proof for this workflow-only guard change.

Persistent data-model note: no persistent data model, vector store, embedding metadata, migration, or runtime state changed. The only `metadata`/`embedding` text in this PR is test coverage text and existing changed-test routing names.

Synthetic edited-event runner log from the proposed branch workflow files:
```text
Synthetic edited-event proof from branch workflow files
head: b480e41f2f3dc9973249b787f98039c0570290ad
event_name: pull_request_target
actor: zats

auto-response
  file: .github/workflows/auto-response.yml
  job_if: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request_target' || github.event.action != 'edited' || github.event.changes.title || github.event.changes.body || github.event.changes.base }}
  opened: success
  title edit: success
  body edit: success
  base edit: success
  metadata edit: skipped

dispatch
  file: .github/workflows/clawsweeper-dispatch.yml
  job_if: ${{ ( github.event_name == 'issue_comment' || !( endsWith(github.actor, '[bot]') && (github.event.action == 'labeled' || github.event.action == 'unlabeled') ) ) && ( github.event_name != 'pull_request_target' || github.event.action != 'edited' || github.event.changes.title || github.event.changes.body || github.event.changes.base ) }}
  opened: success
  title edit: success
  body edit: success
  base edit: success
  metadata edit: skipped

label
  file: .github/workflows/labeler.yml
  job_if: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request_target' && ( github.event.action != 'edited' || github.event.changes.title || github.event.changes.base ) }}
  opened: success
  title edit: success
  body edit: skipped
  base edit: success
  metadata edit: skipped

Real behavior proof
  file: .github/workflows/real-behavior-proof.yml
  job_if: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request_target' || github.event.action != 'edited' || github.event.changes.body || github.event.changes.base }}
  opened: success
  title edit: skipped
  body edit: success
  base edit: success
  metadata edit: skipped

Labeler edited-step proof
actions/labeler
  step_if: ${{ github.event.action != 'edited' || github.event.changes.base }}
  title edit: skipped
  body edit: skipped
  base edit: success
  metadata edit: skipped

Apply PR size label
  step_if: ${{ github.event.action != 'edited' || github.event.changes.base }}
  title edit: skipped
  body edit: skipped
  base edit: success
  metadata edit: skipped

Apply maintainer or trusted-contributor label
  step_if: ${{ github.event.action != 'edited' }}
  title edit: skipped
  body edit: skipped
  base edit: skipped
  metadata edit: skipped

Apply beta-blocker title label
  step_if: ${{ github.event.action != 'edited' || github.event.changes.title }}
  title edit: success
  body edit: skipped
  base edit: skipped
  metadata edit: skipped

Apply too-many-prs label
  step_if: ${{ github.event.action != 'edited' }}
  title edit: skipped
  body edit: skipped
  base edit: skipped
  metadata edit: skipped
```

Data-model compatibility proof:
```text
git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD
.github/workflows/auto-response.yml
.github/workflows/clawsweeper-dispatch.yml
.github/workflows/labeler.yml
.github/workflows/real-behavior-proof.yml
scripts/test-projects.test-support.mjs

Observed result: the diff only touches GitHub workflow files, workflow guard tests, and changed-test routing. No runtime state, schema, migration, datastore, vector, or embedding implementation files changed; migration and upgrade compatibility proof is N/A for this PR.

What was not tested: a full live GitHub `pull_request_target` edited-event matrix using this branch as the target branch. That can only run after this workflow change lands on a target branch.

target.title: fix(memory): default memorySearch provider to "auto" instead of "openai"
target.body:
## Summary

After upgrading to 2026.6.1, the `memorySearch` provider silently resets to `"openai"` when no provider is explicitly configured. Previously (2026.5.26) the default was `"auto"`, which correctly auto-selected an available embedding provider (e.g. `gemini-embedding-001` for Google AI Studio users).

The regression was introduced in commit `4d89e00c50` ("feat(embeddings): add OpenAI-compatible core provider (#85269)") which hardcoded the unresolved default to `"openai"` (`DEFAULT_MEMORY_EMBEDDING_PROVIDER`), bypassing the auto-select mechanism.

Fixes #90787

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: memorySearch provider silently defaults to "openai" when not configured, instead of "auto". This breaks vector search for users without an OpenAI API key — the index identity check compares against an empty string and permanently pauses vector search.

**Real setup tested**: Node v24.13.1 running on Linux x86_64, on a local worktree branch of openclaw/openclaw@main (commit bf872b30cd). Verifying the mergeConfig default value fix that restores "auto" as the default memorySearch provider.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/memory-search.test.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**:

Terminal capture of the actual test run:

```
> node scripts/test-projects.mjs "src/agents/memory-search.test.ts"

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  35 passed (35)
   Start at  23:28:38
   Duration  6.22s (transform 2.82s, setup 0ms, import 5.37s, tests 500ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 16.33s
```

**Observed result after the fix**: All 35 tests pass. The key behavioral changes verified by the updated test expectations:

- `defaults provider to auto when unspecified` — unconfigured provider now resolves to `"auto"` instead of `"openai"`
- `preserves explicit auto provider config` — explicitly setting `"auto"` is preserved through config resolution
- All existing explicit provider tests remain passing: `"openai"`, `"local"`, `"none"`, and custom provider aliases all resolve identically to pre-fix behavior

**What was not tested**: End-to-end verification with live embedding providers (Google AI Studio, OpenAI) was not performed — that would require live API keys and a running OpenClaw gateway. The fix is validated at the config-resolution unit test level (`memory-search.test.ts`), which is the exact layer where the regression was introduced and where the default provider value is resolved.

## Tests and validation

| Test Type | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Unit Tests | ✅ | 35/35 passed |

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes` / `No`)

**Yes** — users who did not configure `memorySearch.provider` will now get `"auto"` behavior (auto-select available provider) instead of being forced to `"openai"`. This restores the 2026.5.26 behavior. Users who explicitly configured a provider are unaffected.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes` / `No`)

**No** — only the unconfigured default value changed; all explicit configurations are preserved identically.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes` / `No`)

**No** — this is a config-layer default value change only.

What is the highest-risk area?
- Users who explicitly set `provider: "openai"` are unaffected.
- Users who explicitly set `provider: "auto"` are unaffected (the value is now preserved through the config layer instead of being resolved at config time).
- The only behavioral change is for users with no `provider` setting — they regain auto-select functionality.

How is that risk mitigated?
- All existing unit tests pass, including explicit provider configuration tests.
- The change is a one-line default value restoration.

## Current review state

What is the next action?
- Maintainer review

target.title: [codex] Add needed sandbox activation mode
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a first-class `agents.defaults.sandbox.mode = "needed"` option for chat-first deployments where sandbox backends such as Docker may be temporarily unavailable.

In `needed` mode, ordinary embedded-agent turns do not preflight or create a sandbox runtime. Sandbox-bound exec activation still resolves `host=auto` to the sandbox, lazily creates the configured backend on first exec, and fails closed if that backend is unavailable instead of falling back to gateway/node host execution.

## Changes

- Extend sandbox config schema/types with `needed`.
- Add run-vs-tool sandbox activation semantics.
- Wire embedded exec tools with a cached lazy sandbox resolver.
- Keep exec fail-closed when lazy sandbox resolution returns no runtime.
- Update `sandbox explain`, doctor/audit guidance, and sandbox docs.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sandbox/tool-policy.test.ts src/agents/sandbox.resolveSandboxContext.test.ts src/agents/exec-defaults.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts src/commands/sandbox-explain.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/bash-command.stop.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`
- `pnpm lint:core`
- `pnpm lint:docs`
- `git diff --check`

Fixes #80296
